repoNote: Repository HEAD is at a later commit. All verification performed via git show at the specified commit 65412d25. A subsequent commit (b598d84ec8) reverted ContentAccessSettings from sealed class back to struct and removed == / != operators — the doc is correct for the 65412d25 commit scope.
previousAttemptCarryForward: Attempt 2 was APPROVED with all 23 claims verified and 7 code examples correct. Attempt 3 applies 3 style-only fixes. This review verifies no accuracy regressions were introduced by those fixes.

styleFixRegressionCheck.redirectFromFix: NOT AN ACCURACY CONCERN — redirect_from changed from [] to 'x/reference_content_access_settings_xp'. This is a front matter routing value, not a technical claim about software behavior.
styleFixRegressionCheck.namedParameterSyntaxFix: NO REGRESSION — Line 256 changed from positional 'new CreateContentItemParameters("Article.News", "news-article-1", "News Article", "en")' to named 'new CreateContentItemParameters(contentTypeName: "Article.News", name: "news-article-1", displayName: "News Article", languageName: "en")'. The parameter names contentTypeName, name, displayName, languageName match the actual constructor signature at CreateContentItemParameters.cs. The deprecated 4-param constructor exists at 65412d25 and remains functional.
styleFixRegressionCheck.pageLinkIdentifierFix: NO REGRESSION — Line 296 changed from 'member_roles_developer_guide_xp' (nonexistent) to 'member_roles_xp' with linkText='Member roles'. Verified: src/_documentation/_documentation/developers-and-admins/development/registration-and-authentication/member-roles.md has identifier: member_roles_xp. The cross-reference now resolves to a real page.

claims.claim: ContentAccessSettings changed from a struct to a sealed class in version 31.3
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs line 11: 'public sealed class ContentAccessSettings : IEquatable<ContentAccessSettings>'. [ObsoleteSince(31, 3)] on IsSecured properties in related classes confirms version 31.3.

claims.claim: default(ContentAccessSettings) now returns null instead of a zeroed struct
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings is a sealed class (reference type). default of a reference type is null in C#. Previously as a struct, default produced a zeroed struct.

claims.claim: ContentAccessSettings instances are reference types
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs: 'public sealed class ContentAccessSettings' — class is a reference type.

claims.claim: The == and != operators now use value-based comparison through operator overloads
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs lines 134-142: operator == calls Equals(left, right); operator != calls !Equals(left, right). Equals() compares IsSecured and RequiredMemberRoleIDs contents.

claims.claim: Namespace is CMS.ContentEngine
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs line 5: 'namespace CMS.ContentEngine'

claims.claim: ContentAccessSettings : IEquatable<ContentAccessSettings>
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs line 11: 'public sealed class ContentAccessSettings : IEquatable<ContentAccessSettings>'

claims.claim: IsSecured property type is bool, read-only
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs line 16: 'public bool IsSecured { get; }' — getter-only auto-property.

claims.claim: IsSecured returns false for Public(), true for Secured() or SecuredWithRoles()
claims.evidence: Public() calls new(false) → IsSecured = false. Secured() calls new(true) → IsSecured = true. SecuredWithRoles() calls new(true, roleIds) → IsSecured = true.

claims.claim: RequiredMemberRoleIDs type is IReadOnlyCollection<int>, read-only
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs line 22: 'public IReadOnlyCollection<int> RequiredMemberRoleIDs { get; }' — getter-only.

claims.claim: RequiredMemberRoleIDs returns empty collection for Public() or Secured()
claims.evidence: Private constructor (line 47): RequiredMemberRoleIDs = [] (empty). Both Public() and Secured() call new(bool) which uses this constructor.

claims.claim: RequiredMemberRoleIDs returns deduplicated, read-only collection for SecuredWithRoles()
claims.evidence: Internal constructor (line 66): requiredMemberRoleIDs?.Distinct().ToList() → .AsReadOnly(). SecuredWithRoles() passes roleIds to new(true, roleIds) which hits internal constructor.

claims.claim: SecuredWithRoles parameter type is IEnumerable<int>
claims.location: Section: Static factory methods — SecuredWithRoles, line 128/136
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs line 80: 'public static ContentAccessSettings SecuredWithRoles(IEnumerable<int> requiredMemberRoleIDs)'

claims.claim: SecuredWithRoles throws ArgumentNullException when requiredMemberRoleIDs is null
claims.location: Section: Static factory methods — Exceptions table, line 142
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs line 82: 'ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(requiredMemberRoleIDs)'

claims.claim: SecuredWithRoles throws ArgumentException when requiredMemberRoleIDs is empty
claims.location: Section: Static factory methods — Exceptions table, line 143
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs lines 84-87: if (roleIds.Count == 0) throw new ArgumentException("At least one role ID must be specified.", ...)

claims.claim: Duplicate role IDs in the input are automatically deduplicated
claims.location: Section: Static factory methods — Remarks, line 147
claims.evidence: Internal constructor line 66: requiredMemberRoleIDs?.Distinct().ToList(). SecuredWithRoles calls new(true, roleIds) which hits internal constructor.

claims.claim: Access mode comparison table — Public: IsSecured=false, RequiredMemberRoleIDs=Empty
claims.evidence: Public() → new(false) → IsSecured=false, RequiredMemberRoleIDs=[].

claims.claim: Access mode comparison table — Secured: IsSecured=true, RequiredMemberRoleIDs=Empty
claims.evidence: Secured() → new(true) → IsSecured=true, RequiredMemberRoleIDs=[].

claims.claim: Access mode comparison table — SecuredWithRoles: IsSecured=true, RequiredMemberRoleIDs contains the specified role IDs
claims.evidence: SecuredWithRoles(ids) → new(true, roleIds) → IsSecured=true, RequiredMemberRoleIDs=roleIds.Distinct().AsReadOnly().

claims.claim: ContentAccessSettings implements IEquatable<ContentAccessSettings> and provides overloaded == and != operators
claims.evidence: Class declaration implements IEquatable<ContentAccessSettings>. Lines 134-142: operator == and operator != defined.

claims.claim: Equality comparison is order-independent on RequiredMemberRoleIDs
claims.evidence: Equals() method line 110: RequiredMemberRoleIDs.OrderBy(id => id).SequenceEqual(other.RequiredMemberRoleIDs.OrderBy(id => id)). Sorting before comparison makes it order-independent.

claims.claim: Equals(ContentAccessSettings other) returns false if other is null, true if same reference, otherwise compares IsSecured and RequiredMemberRoleIDs
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs lines 97-110: null check → false, ReferenceEquals → true, then compares IsSecured and ordered RequiredMemberRoleIDs.

claims.claim: Equals(object obj) returns false if obj is not a ContentAccessSettings instance
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs line 113: 'Equals(obj as ContentAccessSettings)' — 'as' returns null for non-matching types, Equals(null) returns false.

claims.claim: GetHashCode returns hash based on IsSecured and sorted RequiredMemberRoleIDs
claims.evidence: ContentAccessSettings.cs lines 117-127: HashCode.Add(IsSecured) then iterates RequiredMemberRoleIDs.OrderBy(id => id) adding each to hash.

claims.claim: Setting AccessSettings to null throws ArgumentNullException on all 8 content engine event data state classes
claims.evidence: 8 content engine event data state files found (Create, CreateLanguageVariant, Delete, Publish, Unpublish, UpdateDraft, UpdateLanguageMetadata, UpdateMetadata). Verified CreateContentItemEventDataState.cs uses ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(value) on AccessSettings setter.

claims.claim: Setting AccessSettings to null throws ArgumentNullException on all 6 website event data state classes
claims.evidence: Code analysis AREA-004 identifies 6 website event data states with null validation: Delete, Move, Publish, Unpublish, UpdateSecuritySettings, UpdateTreePathSlug. Verified pattern in source files.

claims.claim: ContentItemMetadata — the AccessSettings set accessor validates non-null
claims.evidence: ContentItemMetadata.cs: 'set { ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(value); accessSettings = value; }' — uses 'set' accessor (not 'init').

claims.claim: CreateContentItemParameters — the AccessSettings setter validates non-null
claims.evidence: CreateContentItemParameters.cs (public): 'set { ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(value); accessSettings = value; }'

claims.claim: WebPageMetadata — the AccessSettings init accessor validates non-null
claims.evidence: WebPageMetadata.cs: 'init { ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(value); accessSettings = value; }' — uses 'init' accessor.

claims.claim: CreateWebPageParameters — the AccessSettings setter validates non-null
claims.evidence: CreateWebPageParameters.cs: 'set { ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(value); accessSettings = value; }'

claims.claim: CreateContentItemParameters 4-param constructor with named params (contentTypeName, name, displayName, languageName)
claims.location: Section: Usage examples — content item creation, line 256
claims.evidence: CreateContentItemParameters.cs: 'public CreateContentItemParameters(string contentTypeName, string name, string displayName, string languageName)' — deprecated [ObsoleteSince(30,0)] but functional. Named params contentTypeName:, name:, displayName:, languageName: match actual parameter names.

claims.claim: ContentItemEvents.Create.Before event handler can set args.AccessSettings and read args.ContentTypeName
claims.location: Section: Usage examples — event handler, lines 269-276
claims.evidence: ContentItemEvents.cs: 'public static AdvancedContentItemEventHandler<CreateContentItemEventArgs> Create'. CreateContentItemEventArgs has: 'public ContentAccessSettings AccessSettings { get; set; }' and 'public string ContentTypeName { get; init; }'. The handler reads ContentTypeName (getter works on init) and writes AccessSettings (set accessor).

claims.claim: page_link cross-reference to member_roles_xp resolves to the Member roles developer guide
claims.evidence: member-roles.md front matter contains 'identifier: member_roles_xp'. File located at developers-and-admins/development/registration-and-authentication/member-roles.md.

codeExamples.notes: Three factory method calls match source: Public(), Secured(), SecuredWithRoles(IEnumerable<int>). new[] { 1, 5, 12 } is valid IEnumerable<int>.

codeExamples.location: Section: Breaking change — Before, lines 44-52 (code block 2)
codeExamples.notes: Shows struct-era code patterns: default and .Equals(). Correctly represents pre-migration usage.

codeExamples.location: Section: Breaking change — After, lines 54-62 (code block 3)
codeExamples.notes: Shows sealed-class-era code: Public() factory method and == operator. Both exist in source.
Public() returns instance with IsSecured=false and empty RequiredMemberRoleIDs. Comments accurate.
Secured() returns instance with IsSecured=true and empty RequiredMemberRoleIDs. Comments accurate.
SecuredWithRoles(new[] { 1, 5, 12 }) — valid call, IsSecured=true, RequiredMemberRoleIDs contains 1,5,12. Comments accurate.
Two SecuredWithRoles with reversed role arrays. == uses Equals() which sorts before comparing → true. Correct.
Public() != Secured() → true because IsSecured differs (false vs true). Correct.

codeExamples.location: Section: Null handling — Before, lines 230-238 (code block 9)
codeExamples.notes: Shows AccessSettings = null in object initializer. The setter calls ThrowIfNull — would throw at runtime. Correctly demonstrates the error pattern.

codeExamples.location: Section: Null handling — After, lines 240-248 (code block 10)
codeExamples.notes: Shows AccessSettings = ContentAccessSettings.Public() as the correct replacement. Valid.

codeExamples.location: Section: Usage — content item creation, lines 254-262 (code block 11)
codeExamples.notes: Uses named parameter syntax: new CreateContentItemParameters(contentTypeName: ..., name: ..., displayName: ..., languageName: ...). Parameter names match source constructor signature exactly. AccessSettings set via object initializer with SecuredWithRoles — valid.

codeExamples.location: Section: Usage — event handler, lines 266-278 (code block 12)
codeExamples.notes: ContentItemEvents.Create.Before += handler pattern. args.ContentTypeName is readable (init accessor supports get). args.AccessSettings is writable (set accessor). ContentAccessSettings.Secured() is a valid factory method.
Uses != operator to compare ContentAccessSettings instances. operator != exists in source. Pattern is valid.
